goal: Plan upcoming trip with a balance of planned activities and relaxed time; include two guided tours and at least one free day; include one hands-on cooking class; organize flights, lodging, and activities; emphasize photography with easy walking routes and good viewpoints.
constraints: Carry-on bag weight limit: 7 kg max.
Photography priorities: easy walking routes and good viewpoints.
entities: Lodging preferences: Locally owned guesthouses or small B&Bs

Booking urgency for July 9–18, 2026: book photography-focused tours, hands-on cooking class, and small-group guided walk as soon as possible; weekends and prime times tend to fill earlier
Bathroom planning: prefer cafés and other indoor venues for reliable restrooms during sightseeing; use public toilets as backup when necessary; plan breaks to coincide with cafe visits.
Tipping etiquette guidelines for walking tours and cooking classes (country-specific guidance)
Snack planning: lightweight, travel-friendly snacks suitable for long walking days in warm weather, including packing and storage tips.
Jet lag minimization plan for week-long trip (time zone differences)
Photography gear planning: recommended number of spare camera batteries for a full day of shooting (2–3); higher for heavy video or EVF use.
Power bank planning: recommended capacity 10,000–20,000 mAh (USB-C PD); weight ~150–450 g depending on capacity; a 20,000 mAh unit may weigh ~300–450 g.
Weight-aware packing: if limited by 7 kg carry-on, prioritize spare camera batteries and a small power bank; consider total weight ~500 g for gear discussed.
Airline rules: carry power banks only in carry-on; most airlines allow up to 100 Wh without approval; 100–160 Wh requires airline approval.
Cables and battery care tips: bring a short USB-C cable; store spares at ~40–60% charge for longer shelf life; use insulated case to protect contacts.
